_comment: Foundry model catalog used by Lab 09 — Foundry Decision Advisor. Scores are relative (1-10) and illustrative for the demo. In production, replace with live data from the Foundry model catalog + benchmarks API (mcp: model_catalog_list / model_benchmark_get).

models.strengths: Highest accuracy general reasoning, strong multi-step synthesis, reliable structured output.
models.notes: Use when correctness matters more than cost/latency (final synthesis, clinical reasoning).

models.strengths: Very low cost + latency, great for high-volume summarization, classification and routing.
models.notes: Default workhorse. Fine-tunable (SFT/DPO/tool-calling) — the base for Labs 01-03.

models.strengths: Foundry-managed router that picks the cheapest model that can handle each request.
models.notes: Use when a single endpoint must serve a mix of easy + hard requests cost-efficiently.

models.strengths: High-quality embeddings for grounding / RAG / semantic search.
models.notes: Pair with gpt-4o-mini for grounded answers — the pattern in Lab 04.

models.strengths: Knows Sutter-specific facts (copays, cutoff times, policies) the base model cannot.
models.notes: Output of Lab 01 (SFT). Use when answers depend on private/domain facts.

models.strengths: Tuned for warm, empathetic Sutter member tone over cold textbook answers.
models.notes: Output of Lab 02 (DPO). Use when tone/brand voice matters.

models.strengths: Tool schemas baked in — correct tool calls with ~80% fewer prompt tokens.
models.notes: Output of Lab 03. Use for high-volume tool-calling to cut token cost.
